description: About Astro: ASTRO is Indonesia's quick commerce company that delivers groceries and essentials to your door within 15 minutes. Founded in 2021 by a group of seasoned e-commerce executives and is backed trusted international investors, it is our mission to make people's lives simpler and easier by saving them time, energy, and money. Just like real Astronauts that take off into outer space, Astro embodies the values of being fast, bold, adventurous, and technologically advanced. Interested in working for a company that pushes the boundaries and strives to be the best? Join us now to be a part of this ground-breaking mission! About this Position: We are looking for a DevOps Engineer to support and improve our platform infrastructure, ensuring reliable application deployment and system operations. This role will be responsible for managing CI/CD pipelines, operating Kubernetes clusters, and maintaining cloud infrastructure across AWS and GCP environments. The DevOps Engineer will also support database infrastructure and automation initiatives to enhance system performance and operational efficiency. Working closely with the engineering team, this role plays a key part in maintaining system stability, scalability, and cloud security best practices. Job Description: Develop and maintain CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and ArgoCD to support automated and reliable application deployments. Operate and maintain Kubernetes clusters, including deployment troubleshooting, service scaling, and cluster monitoring. Provision and manage infrastructure using Terraform and Ansible following established standards and best practices. Manage and optimize cloud resources in AWS and GCP, including compute, networking, IAM, and storage services. Support database infrastructure operations, including building cluster servers, managing database systems, and assisting with query performance analysis. Manage and monitor database technologies such as MongoDB, PostgreSQL, Redis, and Elasticsearch. Develop automation scripts using Python, Bash, or Go to streamline operational processes and reduce manual tasks. Monitor system health, troubleshoot issues, and respond to incidents with initial investigation and escalation when required. Implement and maintain cloud security best practices, including access control and infrastructure security configurations. Maintain infrastructure documentation and continuously propose improvements to enhance system stability, efficiency, and scalability.

description: Development and maintain the infrastructure: DevOps engineers are responsible for creating and managing the infrastructure needed to support software development and deployment. This includes designing, configuring, and maintaining servers, networks, storage, and other infrastructure components. Automate processes: DevOps engineers use automation tools like Ansible, Puppet, or Chef to automate tasks like software deployment, configuration management, and testing. They also develop and maintain scripts and code to automate processes and tasks. Collaborate with cross-functional teams: DevOps engineers work closely with development, testing and support team to ensure that software applications are developed and deployed smoothly. They collaborate on code changes, help troubleshoot issues, and ensure that applications are deployed in a timely and efficient manner. Implement and manage security policies: DevOps engineers are responsible for implementing and managing security policies and procedures to ensure the safety and integrity of the software applications and data. This include identifying potential security threats, implementing security measures, and monitoring for security breaches. Continuously improve processes: DevOps engineers are always looking for ways to improve the software development and deployment process. They analyze performance metrics and other data to identify areas for improvement and work to implement changes that increase efficiency and reduce errors. description: Two designers. 20,000+ doctors. Millions of patients. That's our current ratio of team to impact. If you've worked on teams where your designs disappeared into endless review cycles, this is the opposite. SeriousMD and NowServing are healthcare products that people actually use daily. We need a Senior UI/UX Designer who can own product areas end-to-end, from research to shipped pixels. About Us Leapfroggr builds SeriousMD (the EMR platform Filipino doctors trust) and NowServing (connecting patients to providers nationwide). Our products handle real medical records, real appointments, real patient care. We're a small team by design. No layers, no approvals queue, no "let's circle back." You'll work directly with our Design Lead, engineers, and founders. What you design this week can ship next week. What You'll Actually Do Own UI/UX design for entire product areas across web, iOS, and Android. Take features from user research through wireframes through polished UI through shipped product. Build and extend our design system — we care about consistency at scale. Collaborate daily with engineering to make sure what ships matches what you designed. Run usability tests with real doctors and patients, then iterate based on what you learn. Present your work, defend your decisions, and stay open when you're wrong. What We Offer Remote-friendly (distributed across the Philippines) Health insurance Flexible hours Paid leave (sick, vacation, maternity/paternity, bereavement) Real ownership — your name is on the work, and the work matters How to Apply Send your portfolio showing your process, not just final screens. Tell us which of our products (SeriousMD or NowServing) you'd want to work on first, and why.
